_schema.purpose: Spec 35 feature-parity exceptions. Covers BOTH (a) audit over-reports (a core capability that has a genuine SGS equivalent under a different name / is not meaningful for SGS) AND (b) legitimately-deferred core capabilities mapped to a Wave. A gap is CLOSED or recorded here with a reason+wave — NEVER silently baselined.
_schema.framework_universal_note: `_framework_universal` suppresses core capabilities that SGS provides FRAMEWORK-WIDE by architecture (not a per-block functional gap). These are (1) styling supports expressed via SGS's no-inline scoped contract + universal extensions, and (2) WP inline-colour / serialisation attrs SGS deliberately rejects (Spec 32 no-inline). This is POLICY-AS-DATA (reviewable here), not a hardcoded dict in the audit logic (R-31-1).

_framework_universal.reason: Provided framework-wide by SGS architecture: styling via the no-inline scoped contract + universal extensions (Spec 32); WP inline-colour/serialisation attrs deliberately rejected (no-inline); plus pure editor/WP plumbing attrs (id/src/blob/placeholder/splitting/tagName/lock/metadata) that are never client-facing functional controls. Not a per-block functional parity gap. Reviewed 2026-07-19.
